description:
After bl-a2ea ships the projection-shape fix for github-issues sync, the existing duplicates need to be archived. Current bl list (43 open tasks) is almost entirely sync-create flood from the broken classifier:

  - 6 copies of 'Fix all remaining map_unwrap_or clippy lints' (bl-9fb4, bl-d72e, bl-b9e7, bl-74ab, bl-9070, bl-50e3) — should map to delivered bl-2fe1
  - 6 copies of 'Fix map_unwrap_or clippy lint on doctor.rs' (bl-dd53, bl-b80f, bl-0242, bl-4fee, bl-415e, bl-66af) — should map to delivered bl-650b
  - 2 copies of each release chore from v0.2.1 through v0.4.0 — all already shipped (CHANGELOG, tags) and corresponding GH PRs already merged

Do NOT just bulk-close. Verify each:
  - The underlying [bl-xxxx] commit is in 'git log main' (release chores: check the corresponding tag exists).
  - The mirrored GH issue is closed on GitHub (some, like #10, are already state:closed but the close-mirror update path is also broken by the same accessor bug — once the fix lands and a sync runs, KnownUpdate emissions should drive those to status=closed automatically without manual intervention).

Then archive duplicates with 'bl update bl-xxxx status=closed --note "dup of bl-yyyy"' and a 'bl link add bl-xxxx duplicates bl-yyyy' so the relationship is discoverable.

Blocked on bl-a2ea — don't run this until the projection fix lands and a clean sync confirms classify now produces KnownUpdate, otherwise the next sync poll will recreate the duplicates.
title: Clean up duplicate sync-created tasks polluting bl list
